# A New Blog, New Job, and ()

(Intro, sections, photos, book club?)

(Intro) Hello! Welcome to the very first of many blog posts

These first few weeks will be a sort of test run before I head off to the UK in September, so expect format and content to change every week until then as I work out the kinks.

On the **19th**, I officially began my summer job as a production assistant for Early Music Vancouver's (EMV) summer festival. I was stationed at UBC's Old Aud that day, where I was in charge of setting up and providing refreshments to the cast of Orfeo (the opera EMV is producing as part of this year's summer festival) as they rehearsed.

During my lunch break, I tried to watch the World Cup final, but the only place on UBC where I could properly watch it was fully booked. I ultimately had to rely on iShowSpeed's livestream to get a picture of the match, and I did manage to catch Spain's winning goal against Argentina, so all's well that ends well.

Since I already brought in the majority of supplies I needed for my job during my first day, setting up refreshments was far simpler on the **20th**, with the exception of the additional items I bought in the meantime.

However, I also helped to move the harpsichord and organ needed for Orfeo from the truck they were in to the Chan Centre. While moving the harpsichord was easy enough, though it needed to be done rather delicately due to how fragile it was, moving the organ required the combined efforts of six people to accomplish, mainly because it weighted 600lbs.

During my lunch break, I had time to take a look around the UBC bookstore and saw a hoodie with a maple syrup can on it. Since it was a clearance item, I gave in to my urges and bought it after I got off work.

Book Club:

I started reading **Values** by Mark Carney a few weeks ago. I want to understand the direction Canada is headed in, and where better to start? Att least he has a plan, even if I disagree with some parts of it.
